description: Refactor CLI (apps/cli) to match Phase 2R API. All responses are now wrapped in JsonResponse.Ok({ ret, data }), enum values are uppercase, and new endpoints exist for labels, ticket assign/update/delete, and project create/delete. Full spec: projects/koda/memory/NAX-PHASE4R-CLI-REFACTOR.md

userStories.title: Response Unwrapping Utility
userStories.description: All API responses are now wrapped: { ret: 0, data: T }. Add unwrap<T>() to src/utils/api.ts and update ALL existing command files (project.ts, ticket.ts, comment.ts, agent.ts) to use unwrap() instead of direct .data access. For list responses use: const { items, total } = unwrap(response). For --json output, always unwrap before JSON.stringify.
acceptanceCriteria: unwrap<T>() exported from src/utils/api.ts: takes { data: { ret: number; data: T } } and returns T
unwrap() throws if ret !== 0
koda project list outputs array of projects (not { ret, data } wrapper)
koda project list --json outputs clean JSON array without wrapper
koda ticket show KODA-1 --json returns ticket object directly
Zero direct .data accesses on API responses remain in command files
All list commands use const { items } = unwrap(response)

userStories.title: Error Handling Utility
userStories.description: Replace all inline catch blocks with a shared handleApiError(err, opts?) function in src/utils/error.ts. Exit codes: 1=general, 2=401/403 auth, 3=400 validation, 4=404 not found. Error messages go to stderr. Include helpful hint for auth errors: 'Check your API key: koda config set apiKey <key>'.
acceptanceCriteria: handleApiError(err, opts?) exported from src/utils/error.ts
Exit code 2 for 401/403 with auth hint message
Exit code 3 for 400 with validation message
Exit code 4 for 404 with opts.notFoundMessage or default
Exit code 1 for all other errors
All catch blocks in project.ts, ticket.ts, comment.ts, agent.ts delegate to handleApiError()
koda project show nonexistent-slug → exits 4 with clear message
No inline error handling remains in command files

userStories.title: Enum Casing Fix
userStories.description: All enum values sent to the API must be UPPERCASE. Update ticket create --type and --priority options, comment --type option, and all hardcoded comment type strings. Add client-side validation with clear errors before hitting the API. Ticket types: BUG|ENHANCEMENT|TASK|QUESTION. Priorities: LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH|CRITICAL. Comment types: GENERAL|VERIFICATION|FIX_REPORT|REVIEW.
acceptanceCriteria: koda ticket create --type BUG succeeds
koda ticket create --type bug → error 'Invalid type bug. Valid values: BUG, ENHANCEMENT, TASK, QUESTION' (exit 3)
koda ticket create --priority HIGH succeeds
koda ticket create --priority high → exit 3 with valid values listed
ticket fix hardcoded comment type is FIX_REPORT (was fix_report)
ticket verify hardcoded comment type is VERIFICATION (was verification)
ticket reject hardcoded comment type is GENERAL (was general)
ticket verify-fix hardcoded comment type is REVIEW (was review)
comment add --type GENERAL succeeds; lowercase --type general → exit 3

userStories.title: Ticket Command Body Fix
userStories.description: ticket fix, ticket verify, ticket reject now require a --comment option that is sent as { body: string } in the request body. Without it the API returns 400. Make --comment required (error if empty). Also update ticket start help text to say 'CREATED or VERIFIED → IN_PROGRESS'.
acceptanceCriteria: koda ticket fix KODA-1 --project koda --comment 'Fixed null ref' succeeds and sends { body: 'Fixed null ref' }
koda ticket fix KODA-1 --project koda (no --comment) → exits 3 with 'Comment is required'
koda ticket verify KODA-1 --project koda --comment 'Verified' succeeds
koda ticket verify KODA-1 --project koda (no --comment) → exits 3
koda ticket reject KODA-1 --project koda --comment 'Out of scope' succeeds
ticket start help text shows 'CREATED or VERIFIED → IN_PROGRESS'

userStories.title: Project Commands — create and delete
userStories.description: Add project create and project delete subcommands. koda project create --name <name> --slug <slug> --key <KEY> [--desc <description>] [--json]. koda project delete <slug> [--force]. Validate --key as 2-6 uppercase letters (/^[A-Z]{2,6}$/), --slug as lowercase alphanumeric+hyphens. --force required for delete. Update project show to display description and key fields.
acceptanceCriteria: koda project create --name 'Test' --slug test --key TEST creates project and displays it
koda project create --key my-key → exits 3 'Invalid key format. Must be 2-6 uppercase letters (e.g. KODA)'
koda project create --slug My_App → exits 3 'Invalid slug format'
koda project create ... --json outputs unwrapped project JSON
koda project delete test --force soft-deletes project
koda project delete test (no --force) → exits 1 'Use --force to confirm deletion'
koda project show <slug> displays key and description fields in table

userStories.title: Ticket Command Gaps — update, delete, assign
userStories.description: Add ticket update, ticket delete, and ticket assign subcommands. koda ticket update <ref> --project <slug> [--title] [--desc] [--priority] [--json]. koda ticket delete <ref> --project <slug> [--force]. koda ticket assign <ref> --project <slug> --agent <agent-slug> [--json]. Also fix ticket show to use ticket.ref directly from API response (not manually constructed).
acceptanceCriteria: koda ticket update KODA-1 --project koda --title 'New title' updates and displays ticket
koda ticket update KODA-1 --project koda --priority HIGH sends uppercase priority
koda ticket delete KODA-1 --project koda --force soft-deletes ticket
koda ticket delete KODA-1 --project koda (no --force) → exits 1 with hint
koda ticket assign KODA-1 --project koda --agent subrina-coder assigns agent
koda ticket assign KODA-1 --project koda --agent nonexistent → exits 4 'Agent not found'
koda ticket show KODA-1 displays ref from API response ticket.ref field

userStories.title: Label Commands — new command group
userStories.description: Create src/commands/label.ts with full label CRUD and ticket label assignment. Register labelCommand in src/index.ts. Commands: koda label create --project <slug> --name <name> [--color <hex>] [--json]. koda label list --project <slug> [--json]. koda label delete --project <slug> --id <id>. koda ticket label add <ref> --project <slug> --label <id>. koda ticket label remove <ref> --project <slug> --label <id>.
acceptanceCriteria: koda label create --project koda --name Bug --color '#ff0000' creates and displays label
koda label create --project koda --name Bug --json outputs unwrapped label JSON
koda label list --project koda displays labels in formatted table
koda label delete --project koda --id <id> deletes label
koda ticket label add KODA-1 --project koda --label <id> attaches label to ticket
koda ticket label remove KODA-1 --project koda --label <id> detaches label
labelCommand registered in src/index.ts (accessible as 'koda label')
'koda ticket label' subcommand accessible via ticket command group

userStories.title: Unit Tests — coverage ≥ 80% for all modified files
userStories.description: Write/update Jest unit tests for all modified and new command files. Mock the generated API client (import from src/generated). Test files: project.test.ts (update), ticket.test.ts (update), label.test.ts (new), api.test.ts (unwrap), error.test.ts (handleApiError). Run: cd apps/cli && bun run test. Mock pattern: jest.mock('../generated'); const mockMethod = jest.mocked(Service.method); mockMethod.mockResolvedValue({ data: { ret: 0, data: <payload> } }).
acceptanceCriteria: bun run test passes in apps/cli with 0 failures
Coverage ≥ 80% for: project.ts, ticket.ts, comment.ts, agent.ts, label.ts, utils/api.ts, utils/error.ts
unwrap() tested: valid response returns data, ret !== 0 throws, missing data throws
handleApiError() tested for exit codes 1, 2, 3, 4
Each new command tested: happy path, API 400/401/404 → correct exit code, --json flag outputs clean JSON
Invalid enum values (lowercase) tested → exit 3 before API call
Missing --force on delete → exit 1 before API call
Generated client mocked via jest.mock('../generated') — no real HTTP calls
